title: SQL: timestamp doesn't obey fractional seconds precision
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_timestamps (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, ts1 TIMESTAMP, ts2 TIMESTAMP, ts3 TIMESTAMP(3), ts4 TIMESTAMP(6));
INSERT INTO test_timestamps (ts1, ts2, ts3, ts4) VALUES (NOW(), NOW(), NOW(), NOW());
SELECT id, ts1, ts2, ts3, ts4 FROM test_timestamps WHERE ts3 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, ts1, ts2, ts3, ts4 FROM test_timestamps WHERE ts4 > '2023-10-01 12:00:00.000000';
key_bug_features: timestamp precision not handled correctly in SQL
